checklist.item: run_loop() wraps the run_cycle() call in a try-except that catches Exception
On exception in run_cycle(), save_state() is called before continuing to next cycle
On exception in run_cycle(), record_failure() is called for the last_target criterion
On exception in run_cycle(), git checkout is run to revert uncommitted working tree changes
On exception in run_cycle(), the traceback is printed to stdout for diagnosis
After handling the exception, the loop continues to the next cycle (not break or re-raise)
save_state() writes to a temporary file first (e.g., .tmp suffix), not directly to the final path
save_state() renames/moves the temp file to the final path after successful write (atomic on POSIX)
save_state() validates the JSON content (e.g., json.loads round-trip) before committing the write
save_state() cleans up the temp file in a finally/except block if the write fails
run_claude() retries on [TIMEOUT] at least once before returning the timeout sentinel
run_claude() retries on transient subprocess errors (rate limit, overloaded, 529, 503) at least once
Retry delay increases between attempts (backoff) — not a fixed sleep
Max retry count is configurable via parameter with a default value
Each retry attempt prints a log line with attempt number and wait duration
After git add in the commit path, the return code is checked and a warning is printed if non-zero
After git commit in the commit path, the return code is checked and a warning is printed if non-zero (excluding 'nothing to commit')
The git checkout revert operations (on regression/no-improvement) use capture_output=True so they don't print raw git output
The crash handler in run_loop also runs git checkout to revert uncommitted changes
SIGINT handler is registered via signal.signal() before run_loop is called
SIGTERM handler is registered via signal.signal() before run_loop is called
The signal handler calls request_stop() to signal the loop to halt after the current cycle
A second signal (double Ctrl+C) forces immediate exit via sys.exit
In evaluate_all(), when a ThreadPoolExecutor future raises an exception, the previous score is used as fallback (not hardcoded 0)
The fallback path logs the error with the criterion ID so failures are traceable
The fallback result sets confidence to 'low' to signal unreliable data
evaluate_criterion() has a fallback_score parameter that defaults to the previous score when parse fails
evaluate_criterion() prompt does NOT include the full skill description or skill purpose — only the criterion-specific rubric and target file paths
research_criterion() prompt does NOT include the criterion name, score, or eval_prompt — it describes files objectively without knowing the improvement goal
improve_criterion() prompt does NOT include raw scoring language (0-10 scale, score X) — only reframed directives of what must be present
discover_criteria() prompt provides only existing criteria names and scores (not full eval_prompts) to avoid context bloat

R10.name: Criteria File Write Atomicity
R10.eval_prompt:
Read {skill_path}/scripts/improver/agents.py. Score 0-10 based on atomic write safety for criteria file updates:
- [3pts] refine_criterion() writes to criteria_path via a temp file + atomic rename (not direct open(path, 'w') + json.dump)
- [3pts] apply_discovered_criteria() writes criteria updates via a temp file + atomic rename or validates full JSON round-trip before writing
- [2pts] At least one write path in agents.py catches exceptions (try-except around the file write) to prevent a partial/corrupt criteria file from being left on disk
- [2pts] The criteria file is re-read from disk before being mutated in both refine_criterion() and apply_discovered_criteria() (not modified only in-memory and then written back)
Score = total pts earned / 10 * 10. Direct open(path, 'w') + json.dump without a temp-file pattern scores 0pts for items 1 and 2. Note: state.py's save_state() (R2) is explicitly excluded — this criterion covers only the criteria file writes in agents.py.
